maplibre.label: MapLibre
externalImports: import React, { useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'

custom_controls.label: Custom Controls
custom_controls.template:
<Map{{props}}>
  <Map.Controls{{Controls.props}}>
    <MapCustomButton
        icon="home"
        onClick={() => console.log('home clicked')}
    />
    <MapCustomButton
        icon="search"
        onClick={() => console.log('search clicked')}
    />
  </Map.Controls>
  <div
      ref={mapContainerRef}
      style={mapContainerStyle}
  />
</Map>
externalImports: import React, { useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'

maplibre_setup.message: Map uses MapLibre: import maplibregl from 'maplibre-gl', include the MapLibre CSS, use mapTheme, and give .pb_map a relative container with explicit height and width.

zoom_controls.message: zoomBtns renders Playbook Map.Controls and the callbacks call MapLibre zoomIn and zoomOut on the loaded map instance.

zoom_and_fly_to.message: flyTo adds the extra control button; flyToClick calls map.flyTo with the default marker position and mapTheme.flyToConfig.

no_controls.message: With zoomBtns false, Map still wraps the MapLibre container but does not render Playbook map controls.

custom_buttons.message: Custom Buttons composes Map.Controls and MapCustomButton children; control props move onto Map.Controls in this structure.
